verses.text: David cries, 'Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God,' set on high above those who rise against him. He seeks rescue and shelter. God lifts His own beyond the reach of their foes.
David cries, 'Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God,' set on high above those who rise against him. He seeks rescue and shelter. God lifts His own beyond the reach of their foes.
Mighty men lie in wait for his life though he has done no wrong; he asks God to 'awake to help me.' His conscience is clear. The innocent may still be hunted, and may rightly call God to act.
Mighty men lie in wait for his life though he has done no wrong; he asks God to 'awake to help me.' His conscience is clear. The innocent may still be hunted, and may rightly call God to act.
He asks God to deal with the proud nations, whose mouths 'belch out' threats as if none hears. Their arrogance is loud. But the God they ignore hears every boast.
He asks God to deal with the proud nations, whose mouths 'belch out' threats as if none hears. Their arrogance is loud. But the God they ignore hears every boast.
He asks God to deal with the proud nations, whose mouths 'belch out' threats as if none hears. Their arrogance is loud. But the God they ignore hears every boast.
'Thou, O LORD, shalt laugh at them,' for the God of mercy will go before David. Heaven is unafraid. The schemes that terrify us are nothing to the God who reigns.
'Thou, O LORD, shalt laugh at them,' for the God of mercy will go before David. Heaven is unafraid. The schemes that terrify us are nothing to the God who reigns.
'Thou, O LORD, shalt laugh at them,' for the God of mercy will go before David. Heaven is unafraid. The schemes that terrify us are nothing to the God who reigns.
He asks God to scatter and bring them down 'that they may know that God ruleth in Jacob.' Their fall is to teach His reign. God's judgments make even the proud confess His rule.
He asks God to scatter and bring them down 'that they may know that God ruleth in Jacob.' Their fall is to teach His reign. God's judgments make even the proud confess His rule.
He asks God to scatter and bring them down 'that they may know that God ruleth in Jacob.' Their fall is to teach His reign. God's judgments make even the proud confess His rule.
His enemies prowl the city at evening like hungry dogs, snarling for food. Their menace is restless. Yet their hunger is never satisfied apart from God.
His enemies prowl the city at evening like hungry dogs, snarling for food. Their menace is restless. Yet their hunger is never satisfied apart from God.
'But I will sing of thy power... for thou hast been my defence and refuge.' He ends in song. The persecuted believer answers threats with praise to his strong deliverer.
'But I will sing of thy power... for thou hast been my defence and refuge.' He ends in song. The persecuted believer answers threats with praise to his strong deliverer.
